content: Autonomous curiosity mechanisms from ICM-driven curious agents can be repurposed to dynamically prioritize which new unlabeled data gets stored and fine-tuned into an active learning agent’s long-term memory, creating a self-organizing memory system that grows only with high-uncertainty, high-learning-gain examples without manual scheduling. This eliminates the common bottleneck of fixed memory retention budgets and redundant low-value data storage in 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent architectures.
